title: Contract Tests, Race and Leak Checks, Chaos, and Release · Go Semantics Case: Compatible Rollout Order
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must layer pure tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable-broker faults, compatible rollout, upgrade, rollback, and recovery proof; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the compatible rollout order increment in a production Go messaging defense dossier. Lea…
objectives: Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Inject return, nack, channel close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, stale generation, race, and goroutine-leak failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Compatible Rollout Order · Theory Compatible Rollout Order

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
